entry.title: cloudalm move events to new configuration

Regards,

Kranthi

entry.title: Postal Payment with Third Party (Outgoing Payments) - SAP S/4HANA incl. Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
This blog post introduces a special payment method - postal money order in Slovakia and Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

When a payer wants to pay their payee through the post office, they first pay at the post office, and the payee can get the payment through this postal service.

In this blog post the focus is the business scenarios with postal services in Czech Republic and Slovakia. When the payment got paid out to the payee. You generate a payment file for the outgoing payment using the payment format.

For the specific payment formats for the postal services:

  • Czech Republic - CZ_PSTLMONEYORDER_B
  • Slovakia -  SK_PSTLMONEYORDER_E

Prerequisite

Business Add-Ins

Based on your business requirements, you can implement the following business add-ins (BAdI), and activate the function modules:

  • Activate the function module FKKID_EVENT_0671 in event 0671.
    Then you can implement the BAdI (Business Add-In) BADI_FKK_PAYM_AMOUNT_ROUND_C for the payment rounding.
  • Activate the function module FKKID_EVENT_0630 in the event 630.
    Then you can implement the BADI (Business Add-In) BADI_FKK_PAYM_CHRG_CALCULATE_C to calculate the additional fees for postal services.
  • Activate the function module FKKID_EVENT_1799 in the event 1799. Then you can implement the BADI (Business Add-In) BADI_FKK_PAYM_CHRG_POST_ENBL_C and BADI_FKK_PAYM_CHRG_POST_PREP_C to post additional document with a total amount for payments and postal charges for bank transfers.
    Note: To post additional documents with a total amount for payments and postal charges, you need to assign the business partner and the contract account. This master data is determined via External Identification in the BAdI implementation. Based on external identification - the Business Partner/Contract Account and assigned Bank details of Czech/Slovakia post offices are used in the documents

Bank Account Settings

Since the payments with money order are not directly paid out from the payer's bank account, instead it is paid out from the post office, it is necessary to create a fictive bank account and account ID for the post office.

Open the Manage Bank Accounts app.
For your company code, create a fictive Bank Account and Account ID for the Slovak post.

Define Payment Methods

Payments to payees through the post office essentially is processing payment. Therefore, you must select a payment method and assign it to the payment medium format.

  1. Open the Define Payment Methods activity using the view cluster VC_TFK042Z in the transaction SM34.
  2. Select the pre-delivered payment formats or use your own configured formats.
    Payment MethodPayment Medium Formats
    P - Money order ESK_PSTLMONEYORDER_E - Postal Payment Order, Slovakia
    P - Money order ECZ_PSTLMONEYORDER_B - Postal Payment Order, Czechia
  3. Assign Postal Remittance for Payment, Slovakia (SK_PSTLMONEYORDER_E) as the payment medium format.

Note to Payee

You can maintain the Note to payee for the payment medium in the Maintain Note to Payee Type for Payment Medium activity using the view cluster VC_TFK042V in the transaction SM34.

Define Parameters for Paying Out

For Postal Money Order B, you maintain sender identification, for example a six-digit numeric value that uniquely identifies the sender. The number is assigned to the sender by the post when the agreement is settled.

Fill in the Customer number in the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions activity using the view TFK012D in the transaction SM30.

Procedure of Generating Payment with Postal Money Orders

  1. Set up the master data of business partner with payee's address only (payee's bank information isn't required). The Slovak Post finds the payees with their address to pay out.
    Set up also the master data for the post office as the following example:

  2. Post a payable item that you need to pay to the payee.
  3. Execute a payment run and clear the payable documents. The total amount of the contract accounting document is posted. You can see the information in the Log details as follows:
  4. In the next phase, the following actions are required for the two entities involved:
    • For your house bank: Run a payment run and generate a payment file for the bank. Your bank then transfers money to the account of the post office.
      Note: This condition serves as a prerequisite for the post office to print out the pay slips for the payee.
    • For the post office: Generate a payment medium file with the necessary information that is sent to the post office. If the money arrives in the post office's bank account, the post office prints out the pay slip and pay to the payee.

Is this blog post helpful to you? Leave comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: Latest People Profile MDF object UI
entry.content:
First of all. Is it possible to configure custom UI for the MDF objects?

The answer is YES. However, it is no longer used the Configure People Profile to assign the created UI. 

Now, this UI configuration is set directly on the object itself. On the "Default Screen" option.

Yes! you must set an  UI (designed at manage configuration UI) as the default screen in Configure Object Definition page. This configuration will impact on the LPP Card. 

 

Does the UI Rules still work on the Latest PP?

Yes, for example the "hide fields" rules still work.

 

Can I show an MDF object twice?

No, Each MDF object can be added only once.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Supplier Evaluation in S/4 HANA - Introduction
entry.content: Hi , Please help with Configuration steps and detail for Supplier  Qualification and Supplier Collaboration...

entry.title: [S/4HANA Private Cloud] Flexible Workflow – Sales Order Remains Blocked After Auto-Skip
entry.content:
I’m facing an issue with Flexible Workflow for Sales Orders.

Requirement

  • ZCS + condition ZP06 : trigger approval
  • ZCS without ZP06 : auto-release (no approval)

Implementation

  • BAdI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON : sets approval reason Z005 for all ZCS
  • Workflow created in Manage Workflows for Sales Orders
  • Custom condition evaluator ZSWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVA

Important Constraint

  • BAdI runs before COMMIT WORK
  • Pricing data (ZP06 in PRCD_ELEMENTS) not yet persisted
  • SELECT not allowed due to transactional contract FUNCTIONAL

 Therefore, launch condition based on ZP06 is unreliable

Workaround Applied

  • Launch condition = NONE
  • Step condition = check ZP06 (via custom evaluator after COMMIT)

Behavior

  • With ZP06 : approval works 
  • Without ZP06:
    • Step condition = FALSE → step skipped 
    • Workflow log = Completed (SWI1) 
    • BUT order remains blocked 

Error

V2468 – Sales document is being processed for approval

Observation

  • No pending work items
  • Workflow WS02000006 = Completed
  • Status in VA03 = In Approval Process

Root Cause (suspected)

If step is skipped:

  • releaseSalesOrder action is never triggered
  • Approval status is not reset
  • Document remains locked

Question

How can we handle this standard gap?

  • Is there a way to force release when no approval step is executed?
  • Any BAdI / enhancement to reset approval status?
  • Or recommended design pattern for this scenario?

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud] Even after deleting the parent item's...
entry.content:
Dear @shiwa,

Yes, if you expect the existing planned orders for the component materials to be deleted/recalculated the component materials also need to be included in the MRP run scope. They do not necessarily have to be entered manually one by one but the MRP run must cover them for example through plant, MRP controller, product group or material scope. Running MRP only for the parent material will not automatically clean up planned orders that belong to component materials outside the selected run scope. SAP confirms that Planning Mode 3 Delete and Recreate only affects materials included in the MRP run. If the material is not included or if there is no planning-relevant change and the material is skipped, existing MRP elements may remain. SAP recommends using Planning Mode 3 together with Regenerative Planning when full reprocessing is required

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3608136

SAP also explains that in MRP Live the planning scope can be defined broadly, for example for all materials/plants or by product groups, MRP controller, or material scopes. During MRP  the BOM is exploded and dependent requirements for components are determined as part of the planning run

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/d2abce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing/exploring-the-concept-of-mrp-and-getting-introduced-to-mrp-live_f71d99ce-e5b9-4dc4-acda-da56f64008ef

Summary:

Do not rely on an MRP run for the parent item only. For component planned orders to be deleted/recreated the component materials must be part of the MRP run scope

Best Regards,

Dawid

Balaji

entry.title: Integrating Business Partner from a S/4HANA Cloud Public to another S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems, Communication Management plays a critical role in securely exchanging data. This blog explains how to configure communication between two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems using standard SAP communication scenarios. The blog is co-written with @Karunaharan. 

 

Prerequisites

  • Access to the source/sender SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
  • User must have the role, ADMINISTRATOR.
  • Target SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system details, such as Host Name, Logical System, and Business System.

 

Step 1: Create A Communication User

Use the “Maintain Communication Users” app to create a technical communication user. Make sure to provide a username and password, as both are required. Ensure that the same user is also created in the target system. This user will be used for sending and receiving data. 

1. Open the Maintain Communication Users app.

2. Choose New.

3. Enter a valid username and description.

4. Either provide a password (basic authentication) or upload certificates for certificate-based authentication.

5. Choose Create to save the new user.

OAuth or Client Certificate can be used if required. 

 

Step 2: Create A Communication System

To enable communication between two systems, you must register these systems in the Communication Systems app (that is, register the target system in the source and vice versa.

For inbound communication, your registered system calls the services provided by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For outbound communication, your registered system provides the services called by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Open the Communication Systems app.

2. Choose New

3. Provide an ID and a name for your system.

4. On the General tab, under the Technical Data section, enter the Host Name, Logical System, and Business System details of the target system.

5. In the Users for Inbound Communication tab, choose Add (+)

a. In the New Inbound Communication User dialog box, enter the username and authentication method.
(created in Step 1: Create A Communication User)

b. Click on Ok Button.

 6. In the Users for Outbound Communication tab, choose Add (+)

a. In the New Outbound Communication User dialog box, for the Authentication Method field, choose Username and Password (the same user must be added as an inbound user in the target system).

b. Click on the Create Button.

 7. Click on the Save Button.

 

Step 3: Create A Communication Arrangement

1. Open the Communication Arrangements app.

2. Choose New.

  1. Select the scenario SAP_COM_0008.
  2. Provide a name for the communication arrangement.
  3. Choose Create.

3. In the Common Data section, select the communication system
(created in Step 2: Create A Communication System)

4. Activate SOAP services using the “Service Status” checkbox.

5. Maintain Replication Model, Replication Mode, and Output Mode. More information about these properties is available subsequently. 

6. Click on the Save Button.

7. Use the “Check Connection” button to verify connectivity between systems

 

More Properties in the Outbound SOAP Service

Replication Modes

Initial Replication: Replicates all existing data from the source to the target system.

Change Replication: Only the changes made in the source system will be replicated to the target system.

 

Output Modes

Direct: Replication happens immediately after saving the changes made to the Business Partner object..

Pooled: Replication occurs periodically. In this case, only the Business Partner objects are replicated without unchanged relationships.

 

System Filter

System Filter controls the data transferred between systems. It allows only certain data to be copied or synchronized based on specific conditions.

For example:

  • Transfer only certain types of data (like sales orders or customer data).
  • Limit the data based on specific attributes (like a region or department).

 

Additional Properties in Communication Arrangement

Data Controller and Purpose

Defines the responsibility and purpose of data usage to ensure compliance with data protection regulations. The combination of data controller and purpose must be the same in both source and target systems.

 

Integration with C4C Enabled

Enables integration-specific services required for SAP C4C integration.

 

SOAP Compatibility Mode

Ensures compatibility with SOAP-based services and avoids message processing errors.

 

Conclusion

Following these steps ensures a secure and reliable integration between two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems using standard communication scenarios.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wien - 07.05.2026
entry.content:
Kapazität: maximal 15 Personen

Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien, um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden.

Diesmal im Restaurant lima56 - super peruanische Küche und ein wunderschöner Gastgarten. 
Hoffen wir, dass das Wetter hält!

entry.title: Request for Guidance on Adding Priority Description in Ticket
entry.content:
Hi Tech Team,

I hope you’re doing well. I am reaching out to request your assistance with a feature we’d like to add—specifically, we want to set a description for the priority of a ticket, right beside the ticket itself. To help clarify our requirement, I’ve attached a screenshot for your reference.

Could you please guide me on how we can implement this, or let me know what steps we should follow? Your expertise would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support. I look forward to your suggestions!

Regards

Md. Aabid Ansari

entry.title: Re: SPPC Revenue Recognition Key for Enterprise Projects
entry.content:
Good day @MananDurge 

Thank you for your detailed explanation and screenshots.

Based on your scenario, I would not recommend that you use the SPPC Periodic Services revenue recognition key for an Enterprise Project as a workaround.

Note that KBA 3656776 only explains how you can manually enter a revenue recognitions key that is not available in the dropdown because it is technically marked as incompatible. It does not mean that every manually entered key will work correctly in the revenue recognition process. Manual entry option is only allowed under specific prerequisites, such as no EBRR postings or cleared EBRR postings.

In your case, the error during the revenue recognition run there an example of one that is not compatible. The system is expecting revenue plan data that belong to the Customer Project or Professional Services process logic, but the object is an Enterprise Project. Therefore, the key can be typed manually, but the follow-on revenue recognition calculation cannot complete because the required revenue planning basis is not available in the same way.

For Enterprise Projects, the revenue recognition key is relevant where a project with revenue is assigned to one or more sales order items. See KBA 3412809 on the relevance of recognition key and its importance for company code, item category, and material combination of the sales order item.

So, the answer to your question is: no, there is no recommended workaround to use SPPC as a Customer Project periodic services key for an Enterprise Project if the revenue recognition run cannot find the required revenue plan data.

The better approach is one of the following:

  • Use as option 1 the correct Enterprise Project revenue recognition key. The key should be derived from the sales order item through the relevant configuration combination. 
     
  • Option 2 is for you to use Customer Projects if the business process is Periodic Services. SPPC belongs to that process logic, so using it avoids the mismatch between the project type and the revenue recognition method. 
  • Option 3 is to review your sales order item setup and its derivation. See KBA 3427750 for more insights.

Also note that KBA 3394591 gives details on the general rules required for you to change a revenue recognition key.

If you also see errors like “Update of Revenue recognition key to master data failed” or “An exception was raised”, is suggest you check whether a project draft exists. See KBA 3263368 for more details.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Link Milestones Billing Plan to billing plan items vi...

entry.title: Re: cloudalm move events to new configuration
entry.content: After migrating to RISE, Cloud ALM assigns a new technical system ID, and event monitoring doesn’t automatically carry over. To continue Integration, Exception, and Job Monitoring, you need to reconfigure them under the new system ID by recreating the monitoring setup manually, since variants aren’t migrated. In practice, this means documenting your old setup and then re‑establishing the same monitoring definitions against the new technical system in Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: Demo Scenario: Clean Core Extensibility with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
entry.content: This looks like an extended version of the demo sample provided here - https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/3051

entry.title: Re: MIRO Header Enhancement with custom fields
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for the Answers. I have added the custom fields using BADI_FDCB_SUBBAS05 under BASIC Data tab in MIRO transaction. Now i need to update these fields using a custom program which calls BAPI _incominginvoice_create to create invoice. My requirement is at the time of invoice creation itself fields should get update in BAPI and MIRO screen. When i check screen BADI i don't find any methods to update custom fileds.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for migration from IS-U to Cloud for Utilities - Brownfield approach
entry.content:
Hi Ivo, 

I just created a Knowledge Based document in our new Industries Learning Group. You will find there description of our Learning Curriculum for Utilities: [Utilities] SAP Learning Curriculum for Utilities - SAP Community. 

We have the S4IUD ​​Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA for Utilities​ training focusing on the delta and transition topics. You can take it as free online course   ​​Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA for Utilities​  or as Instructor Led Training: https://training.sap.com/course/S4IUD. 

I encourage you to register to the group and Knowledge Base Article as I will post updates in the document. 

Thanks,

Greg

entry.title: Re: Best practices for restricting physical deletion of S...
entry.content:
Hi Harukiy,

Correct answer was posted in this question: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/best-practices-for-restricting-physical-deletion-of-sales-orders/qaq-p/14382026#M4941091

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
You can utilize the Migration Object `FI - G/L account balance and open/line item` for your task.  Fixed asset balances are migrated with the Fixed asset - Postings migration object. 

I recommend creating a separate Migration Project and adding this Migration Object to the project. Once added, you can upload the relevant files, complete the mapping steps, and proceed with the simulation process.  

After successfully completing the simulation without encountering any errors, you can move forward with Migration.

 

Thanks and Regards,
SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking SAP BODS .ATL Files: Stop Taking Screenshots and Extract Datastore Metadata with Pytho
entry.content: Hi Rudrapuneet, I found this very interesting as we are currently moving away from datastores per project to datastores per schema so I am having to export the repository atl, make the necessary Find & Replace and re-importing the atl so I would be very interested in the new scripts you have built that extracts the entire ATL file—including all Workflows, Dataflows, Jobs, and Tables—into 9 completely separated, perfectly aligned CSV files. That would be of immense help....  Thanks so much.... Also, do you know how we could use the CLI to export atl files ?

entry.title: Unable to get file picker in pop-up of RAP application
entry.content:
I want my pop-up to be able to pick a file from my desktop but I am not getting the option to do it.
Below is how the popup looks now, when I click upload excel it does go to my method in debugger.
So I think I am on right path.


I am using abstract entity to do this because I really do not want to add a field to my table which would hold the excel file.

@EndUserText.label: 'Upload Excel'
define abstract entity Z_S_ExcelUpload
{     
  @Semantics.largeObject: { 
      mimeType: 'MimeType', 
      contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT
  }
  @EndUserText.label: 'Excel File'     
  attachment : abap.rawstring;
  
  .hidden: true
  @Semantics.mimeType: true
  mimetype   : abap.char(128);
}

what am I doing wrong, how can I make it pick an excel file from my desktop?
Is this because I am using OData V2 – UI instead of V4?
Thanks.

entry.title: What backup options are available in SAP Commerce Cloud and how to restore them?
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud comes with built-in backup capabilities, but the documentation around automated backups and how to actually use them in practice is not always easy to find. This post aims to collect the most common questions in one place.

We are looking for clear answers on the following:

  1. How often are backups created automatically in SAP Commerce Cloud? What types of backups exist and what is the difference between them? What role does Microsoft Azure play in the backup process, and is there any way to interact with Azure-level backups directly?

  2. Where can existing backups be found? Are all backup types visible in the Cloud Portal, or are some of them only accessible through SAP support?

  3. What is the process to restore a backup in case of an emergency? How long does a restore typically take, and does the answer differ depending on whether it is a database-only restore or a full restore including media?

Any information on retention periods, self-service vs. SAP-assisted restore, and limitations (e.g. media not included in certain backup types) would also be very helpful.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What backup options are available in SAP Commerce Clo...
entry.content:
There are actually two separate backup mechanisms running in SAP Commerce Cloud, and understanding the difference between them is key to answering all three questions.

1. How often are backups created and what is Azure's role?

SAP Commerce Cloud uses Microsoft Azure SQL as its database engine, which includes automated backups that are enabled by default and run continuously in the background with no manual action required:

  • Full database backups: every week
  • Differential backups: every 12 to 24 hours
  • Transaction log backups: approximately every 10 minutes

This means the maximum potential data loss in an automated restore scenario is around 10 minutes. These backups are retained for 35 days across all CCv2 environments.

Customers do not have direct access to these backups through the Azure Portal or any self-service interface. They are managed entirely at the infrastructure level by SAP and Azure.

In addition to the automated Azure backups, it is also possible to create backups manually via the Cloud Portal. There are two types available:

Standard Data Backup covers both the database and media files. It does not expire until manually deleted. Depending on the size of the database and the number of media files, it can take up to several hours to complete.

Quick Data Backup covers the database only, leveraging Azure Point-in-Time Restore technology. It can be created in minutes and automatically expires after 35 days.

2. Where can backups be found?

Automated Azure backups are not visible in the Cloud Portal or Azure Portal on the customer side. They exist at the infrastructure level and are not directly browsable or accessible.

Manually created Standard and Quick Data Backups are fully visible in the Cloud Portal under the backup section of each environment and can be managed there directly as a self-service feature.

Backup limits per environment type:

  • Standard Data Backups: maximum 3 in production, maximum 2 in non-production
  • Quick Data Backups: maximum 4 in any environment type

3. How and how fast can backups be restored in an emergency?

The process depends on which backup type you are working with.

For automated Azure point-in-time restore (database only): this is not a self-service feature. A support ticket must be raised with the following information: the database name, the exact point in time to restore to within the 35-day retention window, the reason for the restore, a risk acknowledgement, and approval from the customer side. Response time depends on the ticket priority. For a Very High priority ticket, initial response is within one hour in most cases, and may be less depending on the support agreement in place.

Important: point-in-time restore covers the database only and cannot be used to restore media. If media files were also changed and need to be reverted, use a Cloud Portal Standard Data Backup restore instead to ensure consistency between the database and media.

For Standard Data Backup restore (database and media): this is self-service via the Cloud Portal. Restore time depends on the size of the database and the volume of media files and can range from minutes to several hours. After any restore, a new deployment in recreate mode is typically required to bring the environment back online.

For Quick Data Backup restore (database only): also self-service via Cloud Portal and faster than a standard restore since it uses point-in-time restore technology.

A few additional points worth knowing:

  • A maintenance page is recommended during restore to prevent data from being modified while the operation is in progress
  • Indexes are included in the restore, so no re-indexing is required after completion
  • If a restore is cancelled midway, services will remain down until the next deployment is triggered

Further reading:

For more details on all of the above, the following resources cover these topics in depth:

KBA 3308351 - FAQ for Data Backup and Restore - SAP Commerce Cloud
https://me.sap.com/notes/3308351/E

KBA 3395752 - Point-in-time database restore on Commerce Cloud
https://me.sap.com/notes/3395752/E

SAP Community blog - Quick Data Backup for SAP Commerce Cloud
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/quick-data-backup-for-sap-commerce-cloud/ba-p/14325384

entry.title: BPA - Internal Server Error Issue
entry.content:
So I am using Build Process Automation under free tier and it is giving me an error very frequently after some time when I create a new project in BPA this Issue Comes All my Automation If I want to check give me Internal Server Error and its happening again and again. 
Does anyone know why is this and how can I solve it.

SAP Build #Error

entry.title: Payment rejected by the bank - Amount Wrong - Structure XML
entry.content:
Good morning, SAP consultants.

When I generate a payment for supplier invoices and their corresponding credit notes, the bank rejects the payment because the amount is wrong. However, if I make the payment only for the invoices, the bank accepts it.

Below, I detail the process:

1. Manage Automatic Payments app: as you can see in the screenshot below, we have both the supplier invoices and credit notes to be paid.

2. Monitor Payment app: it indicates that the payment was rejected due to the wrong amount.

3. Manage Bank Messages: the bank has accepted the XML payment structure.

4. I'm sorry, I can't share the payment XML file with you, but if we analyze it, we can see that SAP, besides indicating the final balance due—which matches the payment processed in Manage Automatic Payments—breaks down the XML file by each item and its corresponding balance, and furthermore, doesn't differentiate between invoices and credit notes. In other words, the SAP does NOT group the payments into a single entry.

Has anyone encountered a similar problem?

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,

Dmytro. 

 

#Chris1973

entry.title: Re: Unable to get file picker in pop-up of RAP application
entry.content:
I would hate to have the attachment stored in the database, I just wanna load the file records to table and not store the file anywhere at all.
Edit: Changed to v4 - UI, still no success. 
Below is metadata, I think it should be Edm.Stream insead of Edm.Binary for attachment

<Action Name="UploadExcel" EntitySetPath="_it" IsBound="true">
    <Parameter Name="_it" Type="Collection(...)"/>
    <Parameter Name="attachment" Type="Edm.Binary" Nullable="false"/>
    <Parameter Name="mimetype" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="128"/>
    <ReturnType Type="..." Nullable="false"/>
</Action>

entry.title: Re: IAS: loginName whitespace handling (lack of normaliza...
entry.content: Influence Request

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: hana calculation view wrong visualizations
entry.content:
Display issue is not related to the Eclipse version but to the scaling of your display. I work with 2 screens and the issue only appeared on the laptop one. 

I changed the scale to 100% (laptop display) and the nodes are now displayed correctly.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for document type for Bank statements (...
entry.content:
Dear @SuyashG,

In CBC choose 101024 Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement

 

For MT940 bank statement processing, the key setting is the assignment of the external bank transaction type from the bank statement file to an SAP internal posting rule. SAP documents that in the Electronic Bank Statement configuration, external transactions are assigned to internal posting rules

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/828a6a386d0342319c64d22e24c62518.html

The accounting document type generated during bank statement posting, for example SA, is not maintained directly at MT940 file-format level in CBC and is part of the SAP-delivered standard bank statement posting logic. In Public Cloud bank statement posting rules are SAP-delivered standard configuration. SAP confirms that posting rules are predefined / hardcoded and cannot be changed by the customer

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003374309

Therefore there is no standard CBC setting where we can simply replace document type SA with another document type specifically for MT940/BRS postings. The supported configuration step is to verify whether the relevant MT940 external transaction code is assigned to the correct available SAP standard posting rule in SSCUI 101024. If a new or changed posting rule is required, SAP treats this as non-standard expert configuration and it must go through SAP review

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003357476

Summary:

The MT940 external transaction is assigned to a standard posting rule there but the resulting document type such as SA is not freely changeable in Public Cloud standard configuration

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Exploring the New ITSM Adapter API from SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Brad,

no. this is not possible, this API is just to allow communication between customer ITSM solution (Case and since begining of 2026 Service Request Read) to the SAP Support Backbone.

There is no integration to the SAP Could ALM Implementation.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Impacts of SAP API Policy v4/2026 on existing customer integrations
entry.content: Isn't "except as permitted by the Documentation or otherwise authorized by SAP (e.g., customers may use custom- developed ABAP interfaces in private cloud and on-premise deployments)" contradictory to your statement "regardless of whether the system is Public Cloud, Private Cloud" ?

Since today around 1PM CET we are experiencing package import errors from SAP Build Lobby in all our tenants with the error attached below. We believe this is a regional issue. Is there a way it can be resolved asap?

entry.title: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently looking for a way to check for bounce mails after sending invoices to customers to ensure, that the mail addresses of the reciepients are up to date. 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Steps for Generating the SAML Assertion to read the data from the Successfactors using IAS
entry.content:
Thanks for the guide.

If I want to replicate this, but instead of SucessFactors, I use S/4HANA, bascially I want to consume ODATA using OAUTH authentication, with IAS as my IdP. 

I already created the IAS-OIDC application with authorization code. but I am stuck with IAS-SAML configuration. I am not able to get pass SAML Assertion token exchange.

Any tips or links where I can get this information?

 

Much appreciated.

SABTI

entry.title: Apply Customer Down Payment in Debit Memo Request
entry.content:
We have the requirement to manually created debit memo requests / debit memos and clear these against previously created down payments. 

During the process of creating the debit memo request, the existing down payments are not automatically proposed. We can manage this manually. But never the less we have the need to show on the output for the debit memo, that the existing down payment will be applied and therefore no further payment is needed. 

Is there an option to achieve this. On the DMR I did not find any options to:

  • refer to the existing down payment
  • use a condition type to enter the applied down payment amount
  • anything else. 

Please propose how to handle this requirement. 

Thanks in Advance

Johannes

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SmartRecruiters
entry.content: Very insightful @chetanchavan101. A timely and much‑needed blog, giving a clear overview of the SmartRecruiters integration journey for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content: Thankyou @Mohammed Baser for your time and response. in here the situation is the material is used as component, so i other orders as well, other material used as components have deletionh flag set at plant, client level, but no warning message during the wortk order opening in IW33, but for affected work order gets the warning, i mean those materials also have deletin flag, this also have deletion flag, but that warning messagee comes only for this. and onr more query please.. where do we set a material as a assembly for a work order, is it like we set it to worki order or equipment, or functional location or some other object. so want to know how to set the assembly to a order, so that i can test it. thanks again for your response

entry.title: Re: Hosted Pages for Child Site
entry.content: Hi Kunal, As discussed earlier, hosted pages in SAP CDC are currently supported only at the Parent Site level, and availability for child sites was mentioned as part of the 2024 roadmap. Could you please confirm whether hosted pages are now available directly on child sites, or if they are still restricted to the parent site? I have a requirement where different child sites need a distinct user experience, and I would appreciate your guidance on the recommended implementation approach with the current SAP CDC capabilities, especially if hosted pages are still parent‑only. Looking forward to your confirmation and suggestions on how best to implement this requirement. Thanks and regards, Bhabani

entry.title: Re: Where to find the SAML URLs for Entra / SAP SuccessFa...
entry.content: Hello Matthias, Thank you for your reply and the diferents links.

entry.title: Re: Why do SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC) child sites not have the "Hosted Pages" feature?
entry.content: Hi Kobico, As discussed earlier, hosted pages in SAP CDC are currently supported only at the Parent Site level, and availability for child sites was mentioned as part of the 2024 roadmap. Could you please confirm whether hosted pages are now available directly on child sites, or if they are still restricted to the parent site? I have a requirement where different child sites need a distinct user experience, and I would appreciate your guidance on the recommended implementation approach with the current SAP CDC capabilities, especially if hosted pages are still parent‑only. Looking forward to your confirmation and suggestions on how best to implement this requirement. Thanks and regards, Bhabani

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets and Equipment Synchronize - Automatic creation
entry.content: Hi Mark any news about the topic? is it still a manual activity the integration between Asset and Fixed asset? thanks in advance Piero

entry.title: Re: How to convert TCURR-GDATU to 99999999 - TCURR-GDATU with SAP ABAP Open SQL
entry.content: Technically speaking, the issue is that you cast to NUMC (characters representing numeric digits) which is a NON-numeric value, so you can't use it for arithmetics. If you cast into an integer, that will work.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Solution Architect certification: Essential prep and expert tips
entry.content:

Who to contact for learning.sap.com exam problems?

Support cases are not answered for 10 days, and the last try the promised review was no given.

I wonder if the 6 try license is worth the money when the solution is not working! Now for close to 3 weeks. (

C_ADBTP_2511 in my case - and 3 tries)

entry.title: Re: New Certification Format for SAP SuccessFactors Time...
entry.content:
@Praveen_Kulkarni1 
This type of exam is different from the old certifications that had a specific number of questions, with single/multiple choice and there were sample questions to help preparing, and these questions were not part of the actual exam.

You need to prepare on the areas related to the scope for Time Management in this case, and when interacting with the AI-Roleplay you will be asked about specific topics, but I am not completely sure about the number. Could be around 25 or more. When enrolling to the exam, you can check all the information. 

Hope this will help, 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Request for Support: Vote for 3 Key Improvement Requests (IRs) for Manufacturing Processes
entry.content: @Kerisha123 Glad to see the progress on the first request! I've cast my votes for the attachment IRs as well. Hopefully, other members working with complex BOMs will see the value in these efficiency gains and join in soon.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ - Regular Maintenance - Announcement - 02 May...
entry.content: Thank you, Fancy.

entry.title: Re: BATCH DETERMINATION IS FAILED
entry.content: Hi @MrAdilsonBenevides , in addition to @Amit_SAP's excellent points, I suggest checking your Selection Criteria within the Strategy Records (COB1/COB2). Sometimes, if the characteristics defined in the selection class don't perfectly match the batch attributes in the warehouse, the system will fail to find a valid batch even if stock exists. It's also worth checking if the 'Check Instructions' in Availability Check are bypassing the batch logic.

entry.title: Re: Field AFVC-APLZL available in S/4?
entry.content: Hi @Robert63 , to add to @Ryan-Crosby's point, the AFVC table remains a core part of the manufacturing data model in S/4HANA. While the field exists, if you are revising your interfaces now, it might be a good time to look into using OData services or API Business Hub equivalents for these subsystems. Relying on direct table fields like APLZL is still common, but moving toward standard APIs will make your future upgrades much smoother and align better with the 'Clean Core' strategy.

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/idoc-error-for-same-custom-idoc-extension-segment-between-two-sap-systems/ba-p/13553885

entry.title: Re: Apply Customer Down Payment in Debit Memo Request
entry.content: Implement a custom output form enhancement to fetch cleared down payment amounts and display them on the debit memo output. Optionally, define a custom condition type as statistical if you want the down payment to appear in the pricing section. Submit a Customer Influence request to SAP for future standardization.

entry.title: Re: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no native bounce‑mail handling for customer invoice emails. The system’s “Monitoring Email Transmissions” app only confirms that the message was handed over to the mail server, not that it reached the recipient’s inbox. To detect invalid addresses or bounced mails, you need to rely on external mail server feedback or custom monitoring

Options to handle bounce mails

1) Use External Mail Server Logs
If your tenant is connected to Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, or another SMTP relay, check their bounce reports. These systems provide detailed Non‑Delivery Reports (NDRs) that can be monitored centrally.

2) Custom Integration
Build a small middleware (e.g., SAP BTP app or CPI iFlow) to capture bounce notifications from the mail server. Feed this back into SAP (e.g., update customer master data or trigger workflow alerts).

entry.title: Re: Apply Customer Down Payment in Debit Memo Request
entry.content: Hi @Johannes_Wienho , to build on Lakshmipathi's suggestion, if you are following a Clean Core strategy in S/4HANA Cloud, the custom output enhancement (via Adobe Forms/Output Management) is indeed your best bet for the visual representation. For the accounting side, ensure that the 'Down Payment' indicator in the Billing Document type is configured, though for DMRs, the link is often strictly a financial clearing task in FI-AR (Transaction F-32 or automatic clearing) rather than an SD-driven one. Have you checked if using Item Category TATX (for Down Payments) provides the necessary pricing integration you need?

entry.title: Re: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content: Hi @FSCHMITT-MANTH3Y, to expand on Lakshmipathi's points, if you're looking for a 'Clean Core' compliant way to handle this, the SAP BTP route is highly recommended. You can set up an iFlow that listens for bounce notifications from your mail provider and uses the Business Partner API to automatically flag the email address as 'Invalid' or trigger a 'Data Maintenance' task for your master data team. This prevents the issue from recurring in the next billing cycle.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ - Regular Maintenance - Announcement - 02 May...
entry.content: Hi @Marc_OC1, this Parallel Quoting feature is a significant step forward for user experience. In complex manufacturing or logistics scenarios, sales engineers often need to compare line items across different versions or projects. Having this natively supported in separate tabs—rather than using 'incognito' workarounds—will definitely boost productivity. I'm curious if this update also includes synchronized session handling to prevent data locks when those multiple tabs are saved simultaneously.

entry.title: Re: New Certification Format for SAP SuccessFactors Time...
entry.content:
Hi @Arulgsenthil and Praveen. This shift toward scenario-based assessments is a significant step toward validating real-world expertise over rote memorization. As someone working in the S/4HANA ecosystem, I’ve found that the real value lies in being able to articulate the 'why' behind a configuration.

My advice for the AI Roleplay is to focus heavily on the Case Studies within your Learning Journey. Instead of just completing the exercises, practice explaining the business logic behind a specific Time Management rule as if you were presenting it to a stakeholder. If you can clearly communicate how the solution meets a business requirement, you'll be well-prepared for the AI's evaluation.

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets and Equipment Synchronize - Automatic cr...
entry.content:
Hi @Premkumar_Venka and team. This is indeed a significant change for those transitioning from On-Premise environments. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the focus is on a 'Clean Core,' and currently, the direct synchronization logic hasn't been ported over to the standard configuration (SSCUI).

Beyond upvoting the Customer Influence idea, a potential 'Cloud-native' workaround for high-volume environments is using SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform). You can leverage the Business Partner or Equipment APIs to trigger a synchronization via a side-by-side extension. While it requires a bit of development, it keeps your core clean while restoring that automation. It’s worth checking the SAP API Business Hub to see if the latest 'Asset Master Data' events can trigger your custom logic.

entry.title: Re: I am getting a blank page when I preview my RAP servi...
entry.content: @Andre I am still encountering this issue. Could you please let me know the resolution?

entry.title: Re: Why do SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC) child sites not...
entry.content:
Hi @gerstmaier15 and team. The reason Hosted Pages remain centralized is to preserve the 'Single Identity' integrity across a site group, ensuring a seamless SSO experience.

However, to solve your requirement for distinct branding per child site, you don't necessarily need the feature 'on' the child site. You should leverage the Screen-Set logic where you can pass a siteID or apiKey context to the Hosted Page. By using conditional CSS and localized Screen-Sets managed at the Parent level, you can dynamically trigger different branding and user experiences based on which child site initiated the login.

If you are on the latest 2024/2025 release, check the Console for the 'Multi-Branding' enhancements in the Hosted Pages editor, which allows you to map specific sub-domains to unique UI configurations without breaking the parent-child inheritance.

entry.title: Re: Where to find the SAML URLs for Entra / SAP SuccessFa...
entry.content:
Hi @LOJ Loïc. The most reliable way to find these URLs is through the Tenant Settings of your IAS console rather than searching for generic addresses online.

  1. From IAS to Entra ID: Go to Applications & Resources > Tenant Settings > SAML 2.0 Configuration. Here, you can download the Metadata XML. This file contains your Entity ID, Single Sign-On URL, and the Signing Certificate required for the Entra ID side.

  2. From SuccessFactors to IAS: In the SuccessFactors Provisioning or Admin Center (SAML Outside Services), you will find the SuccessFactors Metadata. Uploading this into your IAS 'Applications' section will automatically populate the Reply URL (ACS) and Audience/Identifier.

If manual entry is absolutely required, the Identifier is typically your IAS tenant URL (e.g., https://<tenant_id>.accounts.ondemand.com) and the Reply URL follows the pattern https://<tenant_id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/acs/<tenant_id>. However, always use the 'Upload Metadata' option in both Entra and IAS to ensure the endpoints match your specific region and tenant ID perfectly.

entry.title: 🎉 📣We are LIVE with the new SAP Security Administrator (C_SEC) Practical Exam!!!!!📣 🎉
entry.content:
Do you remember the days when you would revise every major bullet point from a training course booklet and try to guess what the 80 multiple choice questions may look like (or even going looking for leaks online? Naughty!)? As a job head-hunter, you may have looked at the candidates’ qualifications and wondered if they actually have the proven practical experience enlisted on their resume, or if it is just certified theoretical knowledge?

Well now, in a fast-paced world where knowledgeable experts with practical experience need to stand out from the crowd, SAP have now revolutionized how you prove your expertise!

For those in the SAP Authorization and Security field, the SAP Certified – Security Administrator (C_SEC) exam has officially transitioned to a new system-based practical exam format.

This means you won’t just answer theoretical questions about which transaction code or app is used to maintain Fiori Catalogs—instead, you’ll be tasked with performing real-world security administrative scenarios like creating SAP Fiori Business Roles and managing Authorizations for end users on a live SAP environment. It’s an open-book, real-world challenge designed to prove you are job-ready from day one.

🛠️ Validate Your Skills
Whether you are looking to earn your first badge or prove your mastery as a SAP Security administrator, these are your essential links:
▪️ Prepare with the Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/managing-user-access-and-security-of-sap-s-4-hana-and-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition
▪️ Take the Practical Exam: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-security-administrator 

💡 What is "SAP Certification Reimagined"?
SAP has transformed its entire certification portfolio to reflect the modern AI era. The Certification Reimagined initiative introduces:
▪️ System-Based Exams: Hands-on tasks in a live, time-boxed SAP environment.
▪️ Scenario-Based Assessments: AI-driven roleplays to test your consulting and architectural advice.
▪️ Open-Book Format: You can use SAP Joule, the SAP Help Portal and SAP Learning during the exam - just like you would on the job.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Versions Explorer
entry.content: pretty nice! congrats! 🤗

entry.title: 💡 How to prepare for the SAP C_SEC certification exam 💡
entry.content:
Want to become a SAP Certified Security Administrator and not sure how to prepare for the exam? Come and join us in our "Get Certified" Live Sessions, where expert trainers will support you by helping you understand the new certification exam format and interface. These sessions will explain the learning journey self-study content and guide you with tips for preparation.

There will be a kick-off session outlining the series ahead, which will then be followed by corresponding content review sessions covering important certification-relevant topics from the courses that are part of the corresponding learning journey. 

These sessions complement your self-study, providing you with the opportunity to get explanations and demonstrations from an expert, who can answer questions you have about the content, helping you to be better prepared for the certification.

The kick-off sessions are planned to run multiple times in the year, with the individual topic content review sessions running on a regular basis, so you can sign up to these sessions according to your own schedule.

For more information, check out the link below, where you can also sign up for the sessions.

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-security-administrator

entry.title: Re: How to dynamically determine Warehouse Source Storage...
entry.content: Hello Sushant, I agree with you in terms of ECC, however it seems that Dahan is using a Public Cloud ERP and its WM solution that is embedded into it.

entry.title: Mastering the Market: Seamless FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses
entry.content:
Commodity trading operates at the intersection of global markets, currencies, and constant price movements. While price fluctuations in physical goods are a known challenge, the hidden sting of Foreign Exchange (FX) risk can often be the difference between a profitable deal and a significant loss.

We are excited to introduce a new, integrated function within SAP Commodity Management designed to automate and simplify how commodity businesses handle currency exposure.

The Challenge: Visibility in a Multi-Currency World
Commodity desks often struggle to identify the exact foreign currency impact arising from physical business transactions in real-time. Without a centralized way to track these risks, hedging remains reactive, leading to unexpected discrepancies and manual back-office overhead.

The Solution: FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses
Our new solution bridges the gap between physical commodity management and financial treasury operations. By providing new hedge desk apps on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) integrated with SAP Commodity Management and SAP Agricultural Contract Management (ACM), businesses can now achieve end-to-end automation.

How It Works: The Process Flow
The system creates a "closed-loop" process that removes manual intervention at every critical step:
* Exposure Generation: FX risks are automatically identified and calculated from physical documents (like Purchase Orders) in SAP Commodity Management and SAP Agricultural Contract Management Trading Contracts, for all price and FX Rate fixed in Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) related conditions.
* Automated Hedge Requests: Once an exposure is recorded, a hedge request is triggered in FX commodity hedge desk and trade request is released to SAP Treasury and Risk Management. (Connection to other TRM systems is also possible).
* SAP Trading Platform Integration (TPI): (optional) connected TPI system provides integrations with external trading platforms to receive quotes and execute trades.
* Optimized Hedge Desk Workflow: To minimize hedging costs, FX exposures with identical attributes can be aggregated into unified hedge requests. The end-to-end process from FX exposure generation to send out trade requests is fully automated and governed by customizable threshold controls to ensure precision and efficiency.
* On behalf of hedge scenario is supported.
* Full Circle Visibility: The finalized hedged rate is fed back into hedge requests, ensuring your hedge desk is always filled with accurate information.


Key Process Overview:

Why It Matters: The Bottom Line
This isn't just about cleaner data; it s about operational transformation:
* 90%* Efficiency Gain: Drastically reduce the time spent on back-office activities through automated workflows.
* Zero* Discrepancies: Eliminate up to 100% of unexpected discrepancies by moving from manual tracking to a centralized SAP system.
* Proactive Mitigation: Manage volatility centrally, ensuring that currency swings never catch your margins off-guard.

*based on assumptions from value engineering

Conclusion: Future-Proof Your Commodity Desk
In an era of global economic shifts, waiting for month-end reports to understand your currency exposure is a risk you can't afford. By automating the link between physical contracts and financial hedges, SAP empowers your team to focus on strategic trading rather than manual data entry.

Stop reacting to the market and start mastering it. With FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses, you gain the precision needed to protect your margins and the agility to act on new opportunities with confidence.

@d059642

entry.title: Re: EXCEPTION SEGMENT UNKNOWN RAISED error while calling RFC IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in Biztalk Middl
entry.content: BIZTALK Middleware is Unable to view and import the iDoc lesser than 758 release but able to import the idoc successfully with 758 release.

entry.title: Re: BPA - Internal Server Error Issue
entry.content: Hi I am not sure if this helps but I have had internal server error the wwhole day today for automations that were working properly last week. So I am inclined to think there is a problem with the process automation today from the SAP End. I have opened a support ticket with them anyways.

entry.title: Re: IDOC error for same custom IDOC extension/segment between two SAP systems (version 750->740)
entry.content:
"Error returned by RfcCallReceiveEx while calling RFC"

RfcCallReceiveEx is a function from the old RFC SDK, which has been out-of-maintenance since 2016!
See Support for Classic RFC Library Ends March 2016 SAP Blogs

No need to investigate this any further...

(Also, I don't understand, what this comment has to do with the current blog?! The blog is about exchanging IDocs between two SAP systems, while your error is happening when exchanging IDocs between an (out-dated) MS BizTalk system and an SAP system.)

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content:
Hi @sankets1. To answer your second query: A material is defined as an Assembly in a work order when it is entered in the 'Assembly' field on the Header Data tab (near the Equipment/Functional Location fields) or at the Operation level.

How to set it for testing:

  1. Go to IW31 or IW32.

  2. On the Header Data tab, look for the 'Assembly' field.

  3. Enter your material number there. If that material has a deletion flag, you will immediately see the IM049 warning.

Why it's different from other orders: In your other orders, the materials are likely only listed on the Components tab. SAP typically does not trigger the IM049 'Assembly' warning for standard component consumption—it only triggers it when the material is used to define the technical structure of the work being performed (the Assembly field). Check the header of your affected order; you’ll likely find the material number entered in that specific 'Assembly' box, whereas in your other orders, that field is probably empty.

entry.title: Re: Decision Rule erro: "No Rule Service found for given...
entry.content:
Hi @LarissaH  ,

for anyone who is looking for solution for the issue . after we import the project make sure the project is in "Deployed" status and not in "Released" .  click of "Deploy" and test again it should work fine .

entry.title: Re: Impacts of SAP API Policy v4/2026 on existing custome...
entry.content:
Hi @JOGethmann and m1m. This policy update is the strongest signal yet that SAP is moving toward a 'Zero-Modification' mindset for the core.

To your questions on transition and prioritization:

  1. Positioning & Impact: SAP is positioning this as a technical debt issue. Non-compliant integrations are now 'at-risk' because SAP reserves the right to change or decommission internal APIs without notice in any release cycle.

  2. Transition Strategy: There is rarely a global 'grace period' for APIs. Instead, the strategy should be 'Event-Driven Remediation.' Whenever an existing integration requires maintenance or a connected system is upgraded, it should be migrated to a published API from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

  3. Prioritization Logic: Customers should prioritize based on Business Criticality and Upgrade Frequency. If you are on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, remediation is urgent because you have less control over the upgrade timeline.

Best Practice: For gaps where no published API exists, do not stick with the internal one. Instead, use the SAP Customer Influence portal to request the API or build a 'Clean Core' wrapper using SAP BTP and ADT (ABAP Development Tools) to expose the required data via a stable OData/REST service. This moves the 'non-compliance' from the SAP core to a managed BTP layer, which is much safer for upgrades.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the New ITSM Adapter API from SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Great blog, Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Configuration for document type for Bank statements (...
entry.content:
Hi @SuyashG. Dawid is correct—in the Public Cloud environment, the flexibility to change document types for bank statements is strictly limited to ensure 'Clean Core' stability.

Why it is set to 'SA': SAP delivers these posting rules as standard content. Because the logic is shared across the tenant, allowing manual changes to document types could disrupt automated clearing and reconciliation processes.

What you can verify: You should still navigate to CBC / SSCUI 101024 (Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement) to ensure your external MT940 transaction codes are mapped to the correct Internal Posting Rules.

If 'SA' is a dealbreaker: If your business requirements absolutely demand a different document type (e.g., for specific reporting or filtering), this is considered a non-standard expert configuration. You would need to submit a request to SAP for review, or consider using SAP BTP to build a 'side-by-side' extension that reclassifies the documents after posting, though sticking to the standard 'SA' is highly recommended to avoid future upgrade issues.

entry.title: Re: IAS: loginName whitespace handling (lack of normaliza...
entry.content:
Hi @BFLXR. This is an excellent catch and a very sophisticated observation regarding identity consistency.

From an architectural standpoint, the preservation of whitespace is often a byproduct of ensuring the loginName can support legacy systems that may have used unusual string patterns, but as you pointed out, it creates significant risk for modern SSO/IDP setups.

Why this happens: SAP IAS treats the loginName as a unique string literal. Because it doesn't enforce a specific schema (unlike an email address), the backend treats a space as a valid character.

The Path Forward: MatthiasL is correct—this behavior is best addressed through an Influence Request. SAP's Identity and Access Management (IAM) team prioritizes these 'normalization' rules based on community demand, especially when they impact Identity Provisioning (IPS) and downstream S/4HANA or SuccessFactors correlations.

In the meantime: If you are using Identity Provisioning (IPS) to feed IAS, I highly recommend implementing a Transformation Rule using the trim() function. This ensures that any identity synced from an external source (like Active Directory) is sanitized before it hits the IAS data layer, preventing the 'invisible' identity mismatches you've described.

entry.title: Re: Unable to get file picker in pop-up of RAP application
entry.content:
Hi @sh4il3sh. You are correct that the issue lies in the metadata representation. For the Fiori elements UI to render a file picker in a RAP action popup, the field must be exposed as a Stream, not just binary data.

How to fix this:

  1. Annotation check: Ensure your Abstract Entity includes the @Semantics.largeObject and @Semantics.mimeType annotations correctly. However, for RAP actions, you specifically need the Stream binding.

  2. Refine the Abstract Entity: In RAP, to ensure the field is treated as a stream in the Action's parameter, you must explicitly link the MimeType field.

Try this structure:

define abstract entity Z_S_ExcelUpload {
  @Semantics.largeObject: { 
      mimeType: 'MimeType', 
      contentDispositionPreference: #INLINE
  }
  attachment : abap.rawstring(0); // Use (0) for dynamic length
  
  @Semantics.mimeType: true
  mimetype   : abap.char(128);
  
  filename   : abap.char(128); // Optional but recommended
}

Why it's still not showing: Even with the correct annotations, OData V2 often struggles to render LOBs in popups via Abstract Entities. Since you have moved to OData V4, you are on the right path. Ensure your Service Definition and Binding are refreshed.

Important Tip: In the UI, the file picker only appears if the action is defined as a factory action or a specific instance-bound action that correctly maps these parameters. If the 'attachment' field in your popup remains a simple text/hex input, it means the UI hasn't recognized the @Semantics.largeObject link to the mimetype field. Double-check that your Action definition in the Behavior Definition (BDEF) mentions the abstract entity as a parameter.

entry.title: Re: The electronic signature is not working in the SAP DM...
entry.content:
Hi @SSP1995. Since you’ve confirmed the functionality works in POD 1.1, this strongly points to a change in how Order POD 2.0 handles service registry or data binding for the Electronic Signature Plugin.

Key areas to check before raising a ticket:

  1. POD Designer Configuration: In Order POD 2.0, verify if the E-Signature plugin is correctly assigned to the specific Operation or Action button. Sometimes, version 2.0 requires the 'Routing' or 'SFC' context to be explicitly passed as a parameter in the plugin configuration, which was more automated in 1.1.

  2. Service Mapping: Since the Network tab shows missing routing details, check if the Production Process or the underlying OData service utilized by POD 2.0 has the necessary permissions to access the routing APIs.

  3. IAS/BTP Roles: Ensure that the user roles for POD 2.0 include any new scopes required for the upgraded services, as version 2.0 often utilizes different microservices for data retrieval.

Recommendation: Given that you've identified a discrepancy in the updated logic, you should definitely raise an SAP Support ticket under the component MFG-DM-ME-POD. Be sure to include the HAR file from your Network tab trace to prove that the routing details are failing to load in 2.0 despite working in 1.1.

entry.title: 1H 2026 Release - Snack view for Program displays different percentage complete than within the prog
entry.content:
Hello.  We noticed during 1H release testing that the snack view for a program displays a different percent complete than if we open the program.  Does anyone else think that SAP/SF should make them match vice having to submit an enhancement request?  This will be confusing to learners.

Based on their response to my incident/case - this is working by design.

Here is their response:

I discussed internally with Engineering team.
The behaviour observed in the newly introduced “Snack View” is expected and aligns with its current design logic.
The progress percentage displayed in the Snack View is based on section-level completion, consistent with how progress is calculated in the ILX bite card. This is why it reflects values such as “A out of B sections completed,” and may appear as 0% if no sections are fully completed yet.
In contrast, the Program Details page calculates progress based on overall program completion, which includes all individual items within the program. This results in a different percentage (e.g., 33%) even if no sections are fully completed.
Therefore, the discrepancy is due to two different progress calculation methods being used:
-----Card and Snack View → Section completion percentage
----- Program Details Page → Overall item-based completion percentage
This is not a defect but a difference in how progress is represented across components.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: New depreciation key customizing option not available
entry.content:
Hi @vibhor_bansal. It is important to note that the SPRO path mentioned by @kassettyvijaykiran is only applicable to On-Premise or Private Cloud systems and is not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

How to handle Depreciation Keys in Public Cloud:

  1. Standard Keys: SAP provides a wide range of pre-configured depreciation keys based on the local Chart of Depreciation for your country. In most cases, you are expected to use these standard keys.

  2. Configuration Path: To view or manage these, you must use the SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) or the Manage Your Solution app. Look for SSCUI 102207 (Maintain Depreciation Keys).

  3. Customization Limits: If the standard keys do not meet your requirements, creating a brand-new depreciation key from scratch is often restricted. You typically 'Copy' an existing key and modify allowed parameters (such as the useful life) rather than building new calculation logic.

Recommendation: Check SSCUI 102207 first. If you truly need a calculation logic that isn't supported by the standard keys, you may need to submit an Expert Configuration request to SAP, as modifying core depreciation logic is a high-impact change in a multi-tenant environment.

entry.title: Re: What is a Data Steward?
entry.content:
Hey everyone.  Do you want hear about the day in a life of a data steward from Coca-Cola?  Check out our recent podcast: 

  • A Day in the Life of a Data Steward — Behind the Scenes of Trusted Data

entry.title: Re: GR ISSUE
entry.content:
Hi @Thirusap18. As Lakshmipathi noted, the most frequent cause for this error is that the Post Goods Issue (PGI) has not yet been completed for the outbound delivery.

How to resolve this:

  1. Check Delivery Status: Go to transaction VL03N (Display Outbound Delivery) for your delivery 80015177.

  2. Verify Goods Movement: Check the Document Flow. If you do not see a 'Goods Issue' document linked to the delivery, the supplying plant has not yet shipped the stock out of their inventory.

  3. Post the PGI: You must first perform the PGI (typically in VL02N) at the supplying plant.

  4. Perform GR: Once the PGI is posted and the status of the delivery changes to 'Completely Processed' for goods movement, you will be able to post the GR at the receiving plant without the error.

Other potential checks: If PGI is already posted, ensure that the Movement Type used in your STO (usually 101) is correctly mapped to the delivery item category in your shipping settings.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 | Friday September 4, 9:00am - 6pm
entry.content: Congratulations—this will be an incredible event! I wish I could be there with you all. Kind regards from SAP Community Curitiba, Brazil.

entry.content:
Check out this podcast from Coca-Cola data stewards.  Does this resonate with you?  Give us your thoughts on the your day to day!

  • A Day in the Life of a Data Steward — Behind the Scenes of Trusted Data

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP TRAIL Environment Not publishing any OData S...
entry.content: Same error. The error is still happening.
